2606.21973.title: SPOTR: Spatio-temporal Pooling One-Token Reconstruction for Universal Physiological Signal Self-supervised Learning
2606.21973.paper_summary: Physiological signals such as EEG, ECG, and PPG are widely used in clinical monitoring. Recent self-supervised learning (SSL) methods offer an attractive way to leverage unlabeled recordings, yet they still fall short in practice. In particular, current SSL methods struggle across heterogeneous datasets, often distorting clinically meaningful structures or learning shortcuts from temporal and cross-channel redundancy. Consequently, existing SSL methods often deliver limited performance under linear probing, a lightweight adaptation setting that better matches real-world medical scenarios. Moreover, most Transformer-based SSL models encode a flattened spatiotemporal token sequence, incurring high computation and memory cost, and are typically developed within a single modality. To address these limitations, we present SPOTR (Spatio-temporal Pooling One-Token Reconstruction), a compress-reconstruct pretraining framework that introduces a single-token global bottleneck for physiological signals. SPOTR compresses each waveform into a single-token representation and reconstructs the signal conditioned only on this representation. Meanwhile, SPOTR introduces an efficient spatio-temporal compaction module to reduce computation and memory cost. Pretrained on 20 datasets spanning EEG, iEEG, ECG, and PPG, SPOTR consistently outperforms the strongest baseline under linear probing, improving average AUC by 18.49%, 21.71%, 17.86%, and 4.64%, respectively. Compared with a representative general-purpose time-series foundation model, SPOTR achieves around 78% lower latency and 52% lower peak GPU memory on average. The code can be found at https://github.com/5GYYYYY/SPOTR.
2606.21973.paper_summary_zh: 生理信號如 EEG、ECG 和 PPG 在臨床監測中被廣泛使用。最近的自監督學習 (SSL) 方法提供了一種利用未標記錄音的吸引人方式，但在實際應用中仍然存在不足。特別是，目前的 SSL 方法在異質數據集上表現不佳，經常扭曲臨床上有意義的結構或從時間和跨通道冗餘中學習捷徑。因此，現有的 SSL 方法在線性探測下通常表現有限，這是一種輕量級的適應設置，更好地符合現實世界的醫療場景。此外，大多數基於 Transformer 的 SSL 模型編碼了一個扁平的時空標記序列，導致高計算和內存成本，並且通常是在單一模態內開發的。為了解決這些限制，我們提出了 SPOTR（時空池化單標記重建），這是一個壓縮重建的預訓練框架，為生理信號引入了單標記全局瓶頸。SPOTR 將每個波形壓縮為單標記表示，並僅根據這個表示重建信號。與此同時，SPOTR 引入了一個高效的時空壓縮模塊，以降低計算和內存成本。在涵蓋 EEG、iEEG、ECG 和 PPG 的 20 個數據集上進行預訓練後，SPOTR 在線性探測下始終超越最強的基準，平均 AUC 分別提高了 18.49%、21.71%、17.86% 和 4.64%。與一個代表性的通用時間序列基礎模型相比，SPOTR 平均實現了約 78% 的延遲降低和 52% 的峰值 GPU 內存降低。代碼可在 https://github.com/5GYYYYY/SPOTR 找到。
2606.21973.author: Yiyu Gui et.al.
2606.21973.authors: Yiyu Gui, Mingzhi Chen, Yuesheng Zhu, Guibo Luo, Yuchao Yang
